Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Tunicata; Ascidiacea; Stolidobranchia; Styelidae; Symplegma
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Tunicata; Ascidiacea; Stolidobranchia; Styelidae; Symplegma
Assembly: The assembly of species Symplegma brakenhielmi (kaSymBrak) is based on 169X long read ONT data and 104X Arima HiC data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/).The assembly process included the following steps: ONT reads shorter than 3 kb were filtered out, thus the remaining reads for a total of about 108 Gb were assembled using Necat with polishing performed using NextPolish. Contigs were subsequently filtered with purge_dups to remove haplotigs and obtain a more accurate, non-redundant assembly. Scaffolding was performed by aligning Hi-C reads to the purged contigs using the Omni-C mapping pipeline, followed by YaHS for scaffold construction. Contamination was checked using BlobTools: a total of 315 small sequences were removed as contaminants; 2 large scaffolds (SUPER_12 and SUPER_16) were initially classified as Arthropoda but they were not removed from the final assembly because their coverage and GC content were consistent with the rest of the genome. Moreover SUPER_12 and SUPER_16 showed no Hi-C contacts with other scaffolds in the produced contact map, therefore they were considered false arthropod hits. The scaffolds were processed through curationpretext pipeline to generate contact map, which was manually improved using PretextView. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.
